methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Solideon is a pre-seed stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oluseun Taiwo that has raised $6.5M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of nine private robotics firms (including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity) and one public comparable, though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints for reliable median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, the stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($6.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a standard 2.5x pre-seed to seed step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $16M. This method assumes Solideon's trajectory aligns with typical capital efficiency and risk-reduction patterns observed across pre-seed cohorts, though sector-specific dynamics in robotics may create variance.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $14M to $19M, with $16M as the median point. The range reflects standard pre-seed advancement assumptions applied to the raised-capital baseline, with $2.4M bounds capturing reasonable uncertainty. Key limitations include the absence of comparable pre-seed robotics firms with disclosed valuations, precluding peer-median triangulation. The final valuation remains substantially dependent on founding team quality, technical differentiation, and capital efficiency metrics not captured in this dataset.
